verdictReason: Spec is comprehensive, well-structured, and ready for implementation. All 5 research topics integrated seamlessly. No HIGH severity issues. Average score 4.2/5. All constitutional gates properly referenced.

accuracy.rationale: FR coverage complete (20/20 FRs), no phantom requirements, priorities correctly mapped (P0s prominent). All NFRs addressed with metrics. Constitutional gate references correct (FR-5.1 → Art. I-VII).

completeness.rationale: All 10 sections present and filled. Testing strategy covers unit, integration, and acceptance. Dependencies identified with versions. Files listed with purposes. Risks documented with mitigations. Minor: OQ-5 marked as 'blocking' but description suggests scoping (not a blocker for Phase 5).

consistency.rationale: All FR-*/NFR-*/CON-* references exist in requirements.json. No contradictions between §3 architecture and §5 files. Dependency consistency verified. Research findings (RT-1 to RT-5) properly integrated. Minor: OQ-5 framed as 'blocking Phase 4' but is actually Phase 5+ design decision (scope clarity needed).

feasibility.rationale: Dependencies (Constitution, node.js, jest, CodeRabbit) all exist and compatible. Technical approach sound (surface reconciliation strategy detailed). Scope fits within 12 stories (Phase 8 planning). One minor concern: NLF-1.3 (>80% cache hit rate) requires cache implementation not yet designed in Phase 4 (will be Phase 4 detail during Phase 8 planning).

alignment.rationale: Tech stack aligns (AIOX agents, Constitutional gates from EPIC-9). Patterns match existing codebase (gate structure, hook layer). Architecture fits within AIOX 4-layer model (L1-L4). Naming conventions follow project standards. Minor: EXECUTION-PLAN.yaml referenced as Phase 8 output but not yet created (expected, Phase 8 boundary).

issues.description: EXECUTION-PLAN.yaml referenced in §5.1 but file does not yet exist
issues.suggestion: This is expected — Phase 8 is planning phase. Add note in Phase 8 section clarifying: 'EXECUTION-PLAN.yaml generated during Phase 8 (Planning) after Phase 7 critique approval'

issues.description: OQ-5 ('How should gate overrides be tracked and approved?') marked as 'blocking' but spec describes it as Phase 5+ design decision
issues.suggestion: Update OQ-5 entry: change 'Blocking: Yes' → 'Blocking: No' with note 'Design decision for Phase 6 (Revise) or Phase 8 (Plan). Suggests approval chain pattern in spec §11'

strengths: Comprehensive integration of 5 research topics (RT-1 to RT-5) into architecture and testing strategy
Three-surface agent trap reconciliation strategy clearly documented with priority order, conflict resolution, graceful degradation, and cache strategy
Gate evaluation pseudocode (RT-4) included with early-return priority logic and compound scenario validation
Constitutional Gate (Art. IV - No Invention) enforcement verified: all statements traceable to requirements.json, complexity.json, or research.json
Story mapping (RT-2) correctly cross-references 12.1-12.8 per article + RT-5 gateway stories
All 6 Phase 4 action items (AC1-AC6) explicitly completed in appendix
Traceability matrix comprehensive: every §section traces to specific FR-*/NFR-*/CON-*/RT-* source

suggestedImprovements.title: Clarify Phase 8 planning vs Phase 4 deliverables
suggestedImprovements.description: EXECUTION-PLAN.yaml and barrier synchronization gates are Phase 8 outputs, not Phase 4. Clarify in §5 or add Phase 8 expectations section.
suggestedImprovements.impact: Reduces reader confusion about deliverable boundaries

suggestedImprovements.title: Expand NFR-1.3 cache strategy detail
suggestedImprovements.description: NFR-1.3 (>80% cache hit rate) assumes caching but implementation details (what to cache, TTL, validation) deferred to Phase 8. Consider adding forward reference to research.json cache_strategy section.
suggestedImprovements.impact: Improves clarity on performance SLO dependencies

suggestedImprovements.title: Add cross-reference to Phase 5 gate design workflow
suggestedImprovements.description: §6.3 mentions 'new QA critique checklist' but could explicitly reference RT-1 findings on 'novel pattern' and new checklist design as Phase 5 blocker.
suggestedImprovements.impact: Aligns Phase 5 gate expectations with research findings

nextAction: APPROVED → Proceed to Phase 6 (Revise). No blocking issues. Spec ready for implementation planning in Phase 8.

gateDecision.threshold: Average score >= 4.0 & no HIGH severity issues
gateDecision.recommendation: Move to Phase 6 (Revise) with optional improvements. All 2 LOW severity items are non-blocking and can be addressed during revision if needed.
